mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the risk-free discount rate applied to equity cash flows, mechanically increasing present values and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions that compress consumer spending and corporate earnings expectations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite the unemployment spike, or the signal shows no predictive power in out-of-sample walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous strength in industrial production and housing starts indicates broad-based capacity expansion and robust fixed investment, which lifts corporate profit margins and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a baseline buy-and-hold strategy over a 3-year rolling window, or the interaction term shows a negative coefficient in a multivariate regression of forward returns.

mechanism_narrative: During a monetary tightening cycle, rising policy rates increase borrowing costs and compress equity valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days as discount rates outpace earnings growth.
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns remain positive or statistically indistinguishable from zero during identified tightening phases, or the signal reverses direction when earnings growth exceeds 8% annually.

mechanism_narrative: Rising gross domestic product directly reflects expanding aggregate demand, which boosts corporate revenue forecasts and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The rolling correlation between GDPC1 momentum and 5-day SPX returns falls below 0.10 over any 12-month window, or forward returns show no directional bias following positive GDPC1 revisions.

mechanism_narrative: When retail sales growth decelerates below a critical threshold, it signals weakening consumer demand that compresses earnings multiples and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive or neutral when retail sales decelerate, or the threshold signal generates a negative information coefficient in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in nonfarm payrolls and industrial production confirms a labor-driven supply expansion that sustains corporate profitability, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction effect vanishes when unemployment falls below 4.0%, or forward 60-day returns show no statistical difference between triggered and non-triggered periods over a 5-year walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: In a late-cycle expansion regime, accelerating industrial output reflects peak capacity utilization that temporarily supports equity valuations before mean reversion, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The signal flips to negative predictive power when the yield curve inverts, or forward 5-day returns show no excess performance relative to a market-cap weighted benchmark during late-cycle quarters.
